questions.question: Which of the following describes the output behavior of a NAND gate?
options: The output is high if and only if all inputs are high.
The output is low if and only if all inputs are high.
The output is high if any input is high.
The output is low if all inputs are low.
The output is the inverse of one of its inputs.

questions.question: In a CPU cache, when a cache miss occurs and all cache lines in the relevant set (for set-associative) or the entire cache (for fully associative) are occupied, a replacement policy is used to choose a line to evict. Which of the following is a commonly employed cache replacement policy?

In digital IC design, which technique involves adding extra logic and scan chains to a design primarily to improve the observability and controllability of internal nodes, making it easier to detect manufacturing defects during post-fabrication testing?

To mitigate the performance impact of control hazards (branches) in deeply pipelined processors, a common technique is to speculatively fetch and execute instructions from a predicted path. What specialized hardware structure is primarily responsible for storing the history of recent branch outcomes and predicting the direction and target of future branches?
In the context of digital IC design, what does a "standard cell library" primarily contain?
options: Pre-designed and pre-characterized basic logic gates (e.g., inverters, NANDs, NORs) and flip-flops.
Power consumption profiles for various CPU cores.
Abstract mathematical models for formal verification.
